channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Italy launches legal probe into Israeli detention of activists from Gaza-bound flotilla
item.description: Italian authorities have initiated an investigation into what they are calling possible unlawful detention after Israeli forces intercepted a Gaza-bound aid flotilla and detained certain activists abo...

item.title: Tanzania and Türkiye sign double taxation avoidance agreement to strengthen economic ties
item.description: Tanzania and Türkiye have officially signed a Double Taxation Agreement (DTA) aimed at avoiding double taxation and preventing fiscal evasion on income tax. This important pact makes Tanzania's 13th s...

item.title: Parker looks to secure Galaxy’s survival in the league after cup defeat
item.description: After TS Galaxy's disappointing 2-1 loss to Durban City in the Nedbank Cup final, interim coach Bernard Parker has shifted his focus to the league to avoid relegation. Galaxy had initially taken the l...

item.title: Key British elections this week highlight shifting political landscape
item.description: With elections across Britain scheduled for Thursday, the outcomes will not only determine the allocation of billions in public funds but also shape the political fortunes of leaders from local counci...

item.title: Cochrane Review Finds Anti-Amyloid Drugs Offer No Meaningful Benefit for Alzheimer’s and Increase Brain Swelling Risk
item.description: A comprehensive Cochrane systematic review analyzing data from 17 clinical trials involving over 20,000 participants has concluded that anti-amyloid monoclonal antibodies, designed to clear amyloid be...

item.title: Guidelines to Stay Safe During Extreme Heat in Pakistan
item.description: Karachi recently experienced extremely hot weather, with temperatures reaching 44°C, and the Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has warned that very hot and dry conditions will continue in south...

item.title: Ichung'wah Denies Pressuring Kang'ata Amid Political Disagreements
item.description: National Assembly Majority Leader Kimani Ichung'wah has fired back at Murang'a Governor Irungu Kang'ata, calling out what he describes as 'hypocrisy' in the governor's politics. Ichung'wah denied exer...

item.title: African Organisations Urge Governments to Strengthen Disease Surveillance and Epidemic Preparedness
item.description: Over 20 civil society and community-based organisations from across Africa have come together to urge governments to prioritise epidemic preparedness and disease surveillance. This call was made durin...

item.title: Iran reports missile launch near U.S. frigate in Strait of Hormuz; no damage confirmed
item.description: On May 4, 2026, Iranian media reported that a U.S. Navy frigate was allegedly targeted by two missiles while navigating the Strait of Hormuz near the port of Jask. The report, published by Fars news a...

item.title: California's New Rules for Autonomous Vehicles: How Robotaxis Will Be Ticketed
item.description: The California Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) recently issued new rules for autonomous vehicle (AV) testing and deployment, sparking strong reactions within the AV industry. One of the most intere...

item.title: Pakistan receives Iranian crew and vessel amid US-Iran maritime tensions for repatriation
item.description: Pakistan’s Foreign Office has confirmed that 22 crew members from the Iranian container ship MV Touska, along with the vessel itself, have been transferred to Pakistan following a US seizure. Accordin...

item.title: Key stocks to watch this week: eBay, Coinbase and Palantir
item.description: The article highlights three companies that investors are watching closely in the current market environment: eBay, Coinbase and Palantir. Each of these firms operates in a distinct corner of the digi...

item.title: APC Extends Sale of Nomination Forms Amid Heavy Turnout by Aspirants
item.description: There is serious last minute activity at the Abuja Continental Hotel where the All Progressives Congress (APC) dey sell nomination forms for different positions. The sale wey suppose end last Friday w...

item.title: Graham Linehan accuses Ardal O'Hanlon of abandonment during transgender controversy
item.description: Graham Linehan, co-creator of the beloved British sitcom *Father Ted*, has publicly accused his former colleague Ardal O'Hanlon of abandoning him during the peak of the controversy surrounding his out...

item.title: HRCP Report 2025: Decline in Civic Freedoms and Judicial Independence in Pakistan
item.description: The Human Rights Commission of Pakistan (HRCP) launched its annual report for 2025, detailing significant deterioration in human rights across the country. The report highlighted severe contraction of...

item.title: UAE Leaves Opec to Focus on National Energy and Economic Goals
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has officially exited Opec, a decision that has surprised many in the global energy market. Sultan Al Jaber, UAE's Industry and Advanced Technology Minister and CEO of Adnoc, ...

item.title: Foreigner Spotted Selling Tea and Mandazi Along Iten Roadside
item.description: In a surprising turn of events, a white man, locally called 'mzungu', was seen hawking tea and mandazi in Iten, Elgeyo Marakwet County. Locals noticed him selling from a transparent container and a th...

item.title: Adam Levine Admits to Inappropriate Flirting with Sumner Stroh While Married
item.description: In this story we look back at the 2022 scandal wey involve Maroon 5 singer Adam Levine and Instagram model Sumner Stroh. Stroh, wey be 23 years old that time, come out publicly for TikTok and accuse L...

item.title: Kenya's Bio-Corn Products EPZ Limited Faces Auction Over Loan Default
item.description: Eldoret-based chemical company, Bio-Corn Products EPZ Limited, is set to be auctioned after defaulting on a loan from a local financial institution. The firm, operating on a 15.2-acre industrial site ...

item.title: The remarkable journey of three Indian soldiers escaping Singapore to return to India in 1942
item.description: During World War II, when Singapore fell to Japanese forces on 15 February 1942, around 50,000 Indian Army soldiers became prisoners of war. Among them, Captains Balbir Singh, Pritam Singh and Gangara...
